sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 11.5g. omega 3 is exactly the kind of fat support you want from a meat. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 11.5g. omega 3 is exactly the kind of fat support you want from a meat. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 0g. everything else is lackluster. in this category, carbs barely matter.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 0g. everything else is lackluster. in this category, carbs barely matter.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 24.6g. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
sections.subtitleText: protein is 24.6g. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin b12 at 370% DV, vitamin d at 24% DV stand out most. that is major vitamin support.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin b12 at 370% DV, vitamin d at 24% DV stand out most. that is major vitamin support.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: iron at 16% DV, zinc at 12% DV, potassium at 8% DV stand out most. useful, but still pretty limited.
sections.subtitleText: iron at 16% DV, zinc at 12% DV, potassium at 8% DV stand out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side. small-fish nutrient density is elite because omega-3s, protein, and micronutrients all come together unusually well here. complete protein delivery is reliable because the category is supposed to do this well. easy to centre a meal around because practical meal value matters.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next. fat quality can go sideways fast because some meats are strong until the fat profile shows up. cost and storage can be a pain because practical downsides still count. brings no fibre support because that leaves a nutrition gap the rest of the meal has to cover.

closing.summary: Sardines has a genuinely strong overall case, especially small-fish nutrient density is elite and complete protein delivery is reliable. the downsides are there, but fat quality can go sideways fast and cost and storage can be a pain do not outweigh the upside. best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
